title: Uniface Index Color Mnemonics
text: Uniface Index Color Mnemonics Color mnemonics in the table define the 64 Uniface index color combinations. Arguments Table 1. Mnemonics for Uniface Index Colors Mnemonic Explanation color General color calculate command. color_ n Set to color combination n , where n is an index color identifer from 0 through 63. Description If it is possible, use the color mnemonic to generate (or disable) all colors using a single command. Include the parameter %p in the output string to represent the color number Uniface wants. For more information, see Parameters in the Output Sequence . Each of the color_ definitions corresponds to one of the 64 color combinations (numbered 0 through 63) available. The definition for each color number defines both the foreground and background color. The definition for color_0 corresponds to the default color combination selected at the start of the session, and is also used to reset from a color. If both the general color mnemonic and a specific color_ mnemonic are defined, the specific mnemonic takes priority. The device translation tables supplied with Uniface use the values shown in the table for determining the 64 color combinations. When you define a device translation table, you are advised to map each combination to the same pair of colors. This ensures the maximum level of compatibility with the device translation tables distributed with Uniface. Table 2. Default Uniface Color Codes Color Foreground Color Background Color System default 0 0 Blue 8 1 Green 16 2 Cyan 24 3 Red 32 4 Purple 40 5 Yellow 48 6 White - 7 Black 56 - ^BEGIN_DEVICE ... ’S(I%(p%8))W(I%(p/8))T’ ^COLOR ’S(I0)W(I7)T’ ^COLOR_0 ’S(I7)W(I0)T’ ^COLOR_63 ... ^END_DEVICE Copy Was this topic helpful?
